commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a90337401b1b2c90e8d1538125ff1f555ad22693)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `zhipu` exception to `isSilentOverflowProneModel`, updates focused agent-settings tests/comments, and remaps three cron watchdog phases from pre-execution to execution.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +16. Total +24 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced regressions from source inspection: `provider: "zhipu"` with `api.z.ai` or `z-ai/glm-*` returns false before current z.ai checks, and `context_engine` remapping contradicts the existing pre-execution watchdog regression test. The original zhipu/GLM-5.2 wedge is not proven with real behavior evidence in the PR.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer action is needed: the branch needs code changes plus external real behavior proof, and automation cannot supply the required zhipu runtime proof for the contributor's setup.

target.title: fix: zhipu silentOverflow + cron watchdog stage misclassification
target.body:
## Problem

Dennis (zhipu/GLM-5.2) users experience transcript wedging because isSilentOverflowProneModel incorrectly flags zhipu models as silent-overflow-prone via the glm- prefix match. This disables SDK auto-compaction for those models.

## Root Cause

isSilentOverflowProneModel uses model id prefix matching (glm-) without considering the provider. Zhipu GLM models use a different overflow strategy than the pattern was designed to catch.

## Fix

Add early exit when provider === "zhipu" before checking model id patterns. Updated function JSDoc to document the zhipu exclusion.

## Tests

- Regression tests for zhipu provider with:
  - bare glm- model id
  - glm-4 model id
  - z-ai/ model id prefix
- Existing test "regardless of provider" renamed to acknowledge zhipu exception

## Impact

Only affects isSilentOverflowProneModel for the zhipu provider. No other providers are impacted by this change.
